improvement_directive: Remove the finite-cell known-logging restriction by deriving the clipped-score drift and variance control from primitive nuisance conditions for the full weak-overlap class, including an estimated-clipping stability bound when the logging propensity is unknown. Alternatively, prove a matching no-log upper bound or a logarithmic lower bound on the same finite-cell bracket to make the rate sharp beyond the polynomial exponent.
flagship_directive: Prove a feasible upper bound at the same polynomial exponent over the full weak-overlap class from standard primitive nuisance-rate or smoothness assumptions, including a stability argument for estimated propensity clipping.
critique: The delivered full-class result is only a lower bound at the calibrated exponent; the matching achievability theorem is proved on a much smaller known-logging finite-cell collection with a rare-arm cell-count envelope and an extra finite-VC logarithmic factor. Thus the note does not prove the exact minimax rate over the stated weak-overlap class, and the original faster upper-rate conjecture is explicitly refuted rather than established. The finite-cell upper theorem is nevertheless a real, self-contained rate result with a concrete estimator and a lower construction that matches its polynomial exponent on collections containing the witnesses. The main under-delivery is that the headline object is a full weak-overlap minimax exponent, while the proved upper side is conditional on strong primitive learnability structure and does not handle unknown nuisance functions or estimated clipping instability.
